scores.correctness_notes: The response describes a v4 library API with specific events and parameters, while the gold standard focuses on the v1 interface specification. The events named (certificate_available, certificate_denied) partially overlap with gold standard mentions (certificate_available is mentioned, but certificate_denied vs certificate_invalidated differs). The gold standard emphasizes interface contract and when to request certificates based on revocation/errors, while the response focuses on automatic renewal mechanics. Some overlap but different framing and version.
scores.hallucination_notes: The response describes a specific v4 library with TLSCertificatesRequiresV4, CertificateRequestAttributes, Mode.UNIT, renewal_relative_time, refresh_events, sync(), renew_certificate() — these are plausible but not verified against gold standard. The gold standard doesn't mention these specific APIs. certificate_denied event is not mentioned in gold standard (which mentions certificate_invalidated). These may be hallucinated or from a different version than what the gold standard covers.
